inline_comments.message: cannot this have a working default value?
What does this mean? A port cannot have the value 0, is that some kind of magic value?

Previously it was true, now it is by default false? Should it not be default true? 
Is there any case when the DFC does not use TLS towards the DR?

why is this an input? How is one supposed to know which envs to insert here? 
The only envs used by the DFC I know of are CONSUL_HOST and CONSUL_PORT, should not these be here?

Is this input really needed? Previously it was /var/log/ONAP, does that ever need to change? 
If so, can the default value at least be /var/log/ONAP ?
I do not know how this works, but I think the http port should not be open (and in that case this cannot work) (?)

This blueprint is generated from component specs by the blueprint generator tool and is not under our control. The current tool generates these default values. I guess the tool would need some modifications to support specific default values. I don't think there is currently a way to specify these default values in the component specs.
But this should not be much of a problem since the blueprint asks for these inputs at the time of instantiation.
This is what existed before. The purpose of this commit is to align the component spec and blueprint with the tool. The port should be fixed in another commit.
That also comes from the tool.
That also comes from the tool. I guess some other applications would need this, but in our case envs is empty.
That also comes from the tool.

As mentioned before, this commit is to align blueprint and component specs with the tool. Changing the protocol and port should be part of another commit. And I guess we have not yet agreed for a solution to this issue. So, a new commit to solve this problem should be in the pipeline.

For full compatibility with k8splugin 1.6.0, the types file here should be 4.5.5 rather than 3.4.  This blueprint deploys correctly with 3.4 types file, but undeploying it will fail unless the 4.5.5. types file is used.
I guess this update should be made in the blueprint generator tool, as the imports are coming directly from the tool itself.

Btw, in my environment, undeploying does not fail with 3.4. So, I am wondering what exactly is failing in your environment? See the logs below:

Is it really necessary to have "PM_MEAS_FILES_" in the variable name? I think "feed0_location" is enough, so it follows the same naming convention as "feed0_name". And since the DFC is now generic I also think it is good to keep the references to the Bulk PM usecase to a minimum.
If the PM file handling should be default, shouldn't the default name be "bulk_pm_feed"?

Input variables names are created by the bp-gen tool. What the tool does is that it combines the "config_key" specified under "publishes" in component-specs (i.e., PM_MEAS_FILES in line 14) with the feed number to create the variable name for location.
We cannot have an empty string for config_key as it is also being read by the code, i.e., under application config. Perhaps we could agree on a more generic name than PM_MEAS_FILES, but that would require updates in the code as well.
Done
Ok, that explains it. I didn't see the connection with the changeIdentifier. So then it has to be the way it is.
